management_on_call: Lee Klarich (Chief Product Officer, Q&A only)

exchanges.question_text: FCF sustainability and deferred payment dynamics. How do platform deals affect FCF profile?
exchanges.answer_text: Nikesh: Platform deals are more efficient from a sales perspective — 50-60% of P&L is sales, marketing, and customer support; platform deals find efficiencies there. Dipak: Approaching end of deferred payment transition — not as much left to do, quite far along the journey.
exchanges.analytical_note: Confirms FCF model is maturing; deferred payment drag is near end. Bullish for FCF quality in FY27+.

exchanges.question_text: DeepSeek impact on cybersecurity demand. Does lower-cost AI model change the security landscape?
exchanges.answer_text: Nikesh: Viewed as a phenomenal pivotal moment — DeepSeek at 2% of cost of other models drives experimentation. Security imperative: deploy with firewalls, sequestered environment, AI guardrails. Any new technology requires you to put more security around it.
exchanges.analytical_note: Positioned DeepSeek as a security demand tailwind, not a threat. Consistent with broader PANW AI security thesis.

exchanges.question_text: AI for security differentiation and win rates versus point solutions. Is any point-solution business left?
exchanges.answer_text: Nikesh: Every security company claims platform now — real advantage is platformized customers have harmonized data, can build agents on top of 1,150 customer data sets. Win rates confirmed improving. Lee Klarich: Cortex Cloud connects AppSec through cloud through runtime through SOC — incredibly powerful. The bigger cloud security action is in runtime with agents; not the CNAPP players who won the last round.
exchanges.analytical_note: Win rate confirmation and differentiation claim on data harmonization. Lee Klarich's Cortex Cloud framing signals strategic shift in cloud security.

exchanges.question_text: Guardrails on deferred payment deals. How is management controlling for credit risk and deal quality?
exchanges.answer_text: Dipak: Higher-end deals drive deferred payment requests. Hardware always upfront. Sales compensation and approval guardrails in place. Enabling platformization at scale.
exchanges.analytical_note: Confirms risk controls exist. Hardware upfront policy limits cash flow risk. Approval guardrails reduce adverse selection.

exchanges.question_text: Rationale for merging CNAPP (Prisma Cloud) into Cortex as Cortex Cloud. Why now?
exchanges.answer_text: Lee Klarich: Runtime security requires CNAPP context. Cleaner cloud posture makes it easier for AI to detect real attacks. Best outcome for customers achieved when they connect all aspects of cloud together.
exchanges.analytical_note: Product consolidation is a strategic bet — simplifies the buyer journey, strengthens platform lock-in. Execution risk: can they integrate without disruption?

exchanges.question_text: Product revenue durability. Is hardware demand sustainable given software shift?
exchanges.answer_text: Nikesh: Hardware expected to deliver low to mid-single digits sustainably. Real growth driver is software firewalls and SASE replacing hardware mix. Cloud volume going up faster than data center volumes declining. Strategy is managing transition to higher-margin software.
exchanges.analytical_note: Hardware stable at low-single digits; software mix expansion is the margin expansion mechanism. No guidance cut signal here.

exchanges.question_text: IBM/QRadar partnership update. How is the migration tracking?
exchanges.answer_text: Nikesh: One day a Harvard Business School case will be written on this for the right reasons. This has been a spectacular partner. Asian bank deal: was QRadar customer, ARR went up 5x. It couldn't have been better.
exchanges.analytical_note: QRadar migration contributing >$100M bookings in Q2. Management highly constructive. This is the external ARR contributor that Nowinski flags in exchange 9.

exchanges.question_text: Gross margin pressure and operating margin sustainability. What is driving the gross margin decline?
exchanges.answer_text: Dipak: Gross margin pressure from (1) faster growth of newer SaaS offerings not yet at scale, and (2) one-time inventory write-offs on hardware (~40 bps) — will not repeat in H2. Nikesh: We still outperformed our margin expectations, both internally and externally.
exchanges.analytical_note: Two margin headwinds identified — one structural (SaaS scale), one one-time (inventory). The one-time item should be monitored in Q3 to confirm it doesn't recur.

exchanges.question_text: Net new ARR ex-QRadar appears to have declined year-over-year for two consecutive quarters. If you pull out the $74M from QRadar in Q1, it looks like net new ARR declined on a year-over-year basis for the last two consecutive quarters.
exchanges.answer_text: Dipak: Acknowledged, attributed to lapping a large step-up in cloud-delivered advanced subscription attaches from a year ago. Net new ARR on newer products driving platformization continues to go from strength to strength. Nikesh: Six years ago this was zero. We're very happy it's approaching $5B. We still believe we are on track to get to $15B on NGS ARR.
exchanges.analytical_note: THIS IS THE KEY BEAR CASE EXCHANGE. Nowinski explicitly flags that organic net new ARR (ex-QRadar) is declining YoY for two consecutive quarters. Management acknowledged but deflected to long-term trajectory. This is the metric to watch in Q3 — is it ex-QRadar improving? The $15B ARR target is the long-term anchor but the near-term deceleration is real.

exchanges.question_text: Agentic AI security. How is PANW positioned for agentic AI security as a market opportunity?
exchanges.answer_text: Lee Klarich: Identity is not a magic bullet — also need to secure the AI environment itself, machine-to-machine communications, and agentic actions with AI-enabled SOC. Nikesh: XSIAM sold over 200 times in 24 months — fastest-growing product in cybersecurity. Expects to build agentic detection, remediation, and management within XSIAM.
exchanges.analytical_note: XSIAM positioned as the agentic AI SOC platform. 200+ customers in 24 months is a strong adoption signal. Agentic AI security is an emerging TAM expansion.

bullish_signals: Win rate improvement vs. point solutions
QRadar migration exceeding expectations (>$100M Q2 bookings)
bearish_signals: Net new ARR ex-QRadar declining YoY for 2 consecutive quarters (Nowinski, exchange 9)
Gross margin pressure from SaaS scale not yet achieved
Q3 EPS guidance $0.76-0.77 below Q2 actual $0.81
analytical_summary.key_exchange_note: Exchange 9 (Nowinski/Wells Fargo) is the most analytically important — surfaces the organic ARR deceleration that is obscured by QRadar. This is the bear case the buy-side is watching.
